steps.title: Spot the time progression
steps.content: The passage marks two events with explicit years: 2014 and 2017. This time separation is the key signal that the transition word must express chronological sequence.

steps.title: Match to the correct transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is sequence: Gomez won a competition in 2014, then created a show in 2017 — two events explicitly separated by years. The blank needs a transition signaling that the second event came after the first.
  • Choice A — "Instead,": signals contrast or replacement, so fails — creating a show three years later is not an alternative to winning a competition. ✗
  • Choice B — "For example,": signals that the second sentence illustrates the first, so fails — the 2017 show is a new event, not an example of winning a competition. ✗
  • Choice C — "Later,": directly signals chronological progression, fitting the 2014-to-2017 time gap. ✓
  • Choice D — "In other words,": signals restatement of the same idea, so fails — the two sentences describe distinct events, not the same one rephrased. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
